title: pupalia lappacea
intro: Pupalia lappacea, also known as Puna or Pupalia, is a plant species that belongs to the family Amaranthaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, headache, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and fight infections. In terms of herbal preparations, Pupalia lappacea can be made into a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be consumed orally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
